title: Peucedanum boninense
taxonomy: Peucedanum boninense is classified within the plant family Apiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Apioideae, where Peucedanum boninense is found. The genus Peucedanum belongs to the tribe Peucedaneae, which is a group of plants that are known for their umbels and are often found in temperate regions. Peucedanum boninense is a species within this genus and is native to certain regions. The classification of Peucedanum boninense provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and ecological characteristics.

intro: Peucedanum boninense is a plant species native to Japan, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is commonly known as Bonin Angelica or Japanese Angelica. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of P. boninense include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Japanese medicine, P. boninense is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to make traditional formulations like Kampo medicine.
